content.text:
```mdl
@ Confidentiality (Non‑Disclosure) Agreement – NDAs
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_12_nda_8

open std.strings

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties (BROOKS and CLIENT)
type Party = {
    name: string,                     # e.g. "BROOKS", "CLIENT"
    is_receiving: bool,               # true for the Receiving Party (BROOKS)
    notification_sent: bool,          # true when a legal‑compelled notice has been sent
    disclosed: bool                   # true when Confidential Information has been disclosed
}

@ Confidential Information (any of the categories listed in the Agreement)
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    is_personal: bool,
    is_financial: bool,
    is_product: bool,
    is_formulation: bool,
    is_processing_procedure: bool
}

@ Event that may trigger a disclosure (e.g. legal compulsion)
type DisclosureEvent = {
    legally_compelled: bool,           # true when law, regulation or court order applies
    reason: string                    # free‑form description of the legal trigger
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the public model objects that can be aligned across standards
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – Parties
entity disclosing_party: Party
entity receiving_party: Party

@ Clause 1 – Confidential Information
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

@ Clause 4 – Legal‑Compelled Disclosure Event
entity disclosure_event: DisclosureEvent

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper Functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 3 – Exceptions to confidentiality (simplified to a Boolean placeholder)
func is_exception(info: ConfidentialInfo, event: DisclosureEvent) -> bool:
    # The full set of exceptions (already possessed, independent development,
    # third‑party receipt, public domain, client consent, etc.) is omitted for
    # brevity.  In a complete model each exception would be expressed as a
    # separate predicate.  Here we return `false` so that the default rule
    # enforces the prohibition unless a concrete exception is added later.
    false

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Obligations (Deontic Modality O) and Prohibitions (F)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1.2 – Non‑disclosure obligation
rule O receiving_party_must_not_disclose:
    # The Receiving Party may disclose only if an exception applies or the
    # disclosure is legally compelled.
    (is_exception(confidential_info, disclosure_event) or
     disclosure_event.legally_compelled) implies
    (receiving_party.disclosed = false) always

@ Clause 4 – Notification when legally compelled
rule O notify_disclosing_party_when_legal_compelled:
    # If the Receiving Party is legally compelled to disclose, it must
    # notify the Disclosing Party in writing.
    disclosure_event.legally_compelled implies
    (receiving_party.notification_sent = true) always

@ Clause 5 – Return of material (simplified)
rule O return_material_upon_termination:
    # After termination the Receiving Party must return all material within
    # ten days unless the Client explicitly waives the request.
    # The model assumes a termination event `termination` that occurs once.
    # For brevity we model the requirement as “always” after termination.
    true always   # placeholder – actual temporal logic would reference a termination event

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prohibitions (F) – absolute bans
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 2.2 – Injunctive relief for breach
rule F breach_causes_irreparable_harm:
    # Any breach (disclosure without exception or legal compulsion) is
    # prohibited and would cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party.
    (receiving_party.disclosed and not is_exception(confidential_info, disclosure_event) and
     not disclosure_event.legally_compelled) implies false always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facts – global constraints (no deontic modality)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# The Disclosing Party is never the Receiving Party in this Agreement.
fact disclosing_is_not_receiving:
    disclosing_party.is_receiving = false

# The Receiving Party is always the party that receives Confidential Information.
fact receiving_is_receiving:
    receiving_party.is_receiving = true
```
